mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing output, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO monthly change vs 20-day forward return below 0.05 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A high unemployment rate (UNRATE above 6.0%) signals labor market slack and weak consumer demand, which dampens earnings growth and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or average return not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Ultra-low fed funds rates combined with declining retail sales signal that monetary easing is failing to stimulate demand, raising recession fears and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25% and RSAFS month-over-month change negative
failure_condition: Signal yields positive forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction coefficient in a linear regression is not statistically significant (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate strengthening consumer demand and residential investment, which supports equity prices over a 60-day horizon, especially in a low inflation environment where the central bank is unlikely to tighten.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL is below 240, the Rank IC of HOUST monthly change vs 60-day forward return is not significantly positive over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in industrial production below the 100 index level signals a contraction in manufacturing activity, which erodes corporate profits and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In more than 40% of triggered instances, the 20-day forward return is positive, or the average return is not statistically negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous decline in unemployment and rise in retail sales indicate robust labor market and consumer spending, which jointly boost earnings expectations and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (UNRATE change * RSAFS change) has no incremental predictive power beyond main effects in a linear regression (t-stat < 1.96).

mechanism_narrative: Gradual increases in the CPI (CPIAUCSL) from a low base signal reflation and improving aggregate demand, which supports equity valuations over a 60-day horizon in an environment where inflation remains below central bank targets.
failure_condition: The correlation between CPIAUCSL monthly change and 60-day forward returns is not significantly positive over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate above the zero lower bound triggers expectations of tighter financial conditions, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return following a rate increase above 0.25% is not negative, or the signal fails a buy-and-hold benchmark over the walk-forward period.
